demographics.living_situation: supported independent living unit, with disability support staff

contact_nok.relationship: disability support worker (present at the call)

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: (Reported by his support worker) He is completely overwhelmed, tearing the kitchen apart, and cannot understand that his outing was just delayed, not cancelled. I can't calm him down and I'm worried someone is going to get hurt.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: His community outing today was pushed back a couple of hours because of transport, he took it as the outing being cancelled, and he has been screaming and breaking things for about three hours. Nothing I say is getting through to him.

symptom_narrative.onset: this morning, starting shortly after his scheduled community outing was delayed by about two hours because of a transport problem
symptom_narrative.duration: about three hours and still going
symptom_narrative.character: overwhelmed and extremely frustrated — screaming, pacing, throwing and breaking furniture and crockery; he seems to have taken the delay as the outing being cancelled altogether
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the support worker says it is far more intense and has lasted much longer than his usual upsets — the worst they have seen him
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: constant since the schedule changed; not settling
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: nothing so far — he is not responding to anything staff say or do
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: trying to explain the change to him in words, or anything that reminds him the outing did not happen on time
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: he is sweating a lot and breathing fast, and has given himself some superficial scratches on his forearms from throwing things
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: he has always needed support to manage his emotions and has had upsets before, but never this severe or this prolonged
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: he needs daily support for complex tasks and for managing his emotions, and right now cannot settle himself at all

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: severe intellectual disability, present since childhood, with delayed development and difficulty understanding abstract ideas like 'later'
past_medical_history.how_managed: daily support from disability staff for complex tasks and emotional regulation

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: lives in a supported independent living unit; a single support worker is with him now; he is ambulant but relies on staff for daily tasks and emotional regulation

communication_style.typical_message_style: History is entirely proxy-reported by the support worker; the patient himself cannot self-report, does not respond to verbal redirection, and cannot process temporal or abstract concepts.

access_barriers.other: Severe intellectual disability means the patient cannot describe his own symptoms; a single support worker is managing the crisis alone and is at risk of burnout.

objective_data_offered.value: highly agitated, sweating profusely, pacing around aggressively
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: support-worker visual impression, not a clinical measurement

objective_data_offered.value: screaming, overturning chairs and smashing crockery, completely unreceptive to anything staff say
some superficial scratches and grazes on both forearms from throwing objects; no deep cuts and no obvious broken bones seen
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: seen over video only; wounds not able to be examined or cleaned by a clinician remotely
visual impression only; no measured respiratory rate or oximetry available

objective_data_offered.value: the kitchen is thoroughly disrupted with overturned chairs and shattered crockery on the floor
